title: Soy Garlic Rice
summary: This umami-rich garlicky rice is a great base for so many dishes, top with your favourite protein, side and dressing and you've got a delicious make-ahead meal.

method.description: Wash the rice two or three times until the water runs clear. Roughly chop the shallots into chunks and peel the garlic cloves. Throw both into a food processor and pulse until finely chopped (or finely chop by hand).

Pop a medium sized saucepan over a medium heat and add the butter. Once melted, throw in the shallot and garlic mix and cook for 10-12 mins until caramelised. Add the salt and sugar and cook for a further minute. Deglaze the pan with the dark soy sauce, scraping up any browned bits on the bottom.

Tip in the washed rice followed by the chicken stock. Bring to the boil then pop the lid on and reduce the heat to as low as possible. Cook for 12 mins, then turn the heat off and let sit for 15 mins.
Divide the rice between Tupperware containers and top with your favourite protein, side and dressing and pop in the fridge until needed.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
